subject: DevOps
title: Security as a Shared Responsibility
description: A 60-second lesson on Security as a Shared Responsibility.

hook.text: Security found a critical bug six months after launch. Millions of users were already at risk.

buildup.text: Traditionally, security was a gate at the end. Code passed through audits right before release.

discovery.text: DevSecOps weaves security into every stage. Dev, ops, and security share responsibility from day one.

twist.text: It's not about adding tools. It's about culture. Everyone thinks about security in every decision.

climax.text: Teams that adopt DevSecOps find flaws 10x faster and fix them before they reach production.

punchline.text: Security is everyone's job. Shift it left, share it wide.

quiz.question: What is the core idea of DevSecOps?
options: Integrating security into every stage of development and operations
Running security scans only before releases
